title: A.H.
content:
High was her heart, and yet was well inclined,
    Her manners made of bounty well refined;
    Far capitals and marble courts, her eye still seemed to see,
    Minstrels and kings and high-born dames, and of the best that be.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ralph Waldo Emerson, titled "A.H.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
